map:
CRYPTO DECISION PIPELINE (all paths under C:/Users/persi/Desktop/MCPenny/makecrazypenny). Layering: providers/ (Layer 0 registry: cache+ratelimit+fallback chains) -> servers/crypto.py (capability logic fns + thin MCP @tool wrappers) -> orchestration/crypto.py (single-symbol engine) and orchestration/crypto_screen.py (universe funnel), with pure math in analysis/ and value types in core/types.py.

ENTRY: `async decide_crypto(symbol, *, interval="15m", leverage_cap=None, settings=None) -> TradeDecision` (orchestration/crypto.py:329) = canonical_crypto(symbol) -> gather_crypto_evidence -> score_crypto_evidence -> decide_from_scores(sym, scored, method="quant") (shared, asset-agnostic, orchestration/debate.py:583) -> enrich_crypto_decision (regime + leverage plan).

(1) EVIDENCE. `async gather_crypto_evidence(symbol, *, interval="15m", settings=None) -> dict` (crypto.py:134) fans out 5 concurrent tasks (asyncio.gather, return_exceptions=True; any failure becomes {"_error": ...} under its key, never aborts). Dossier keys: symbol, interval, plus:
- "mtf" = servers/crypto.multi_timeframe(sym) (servers/crypto.py:129): fixed _MTF_TIMEFRAMES 5m/15m/1h x 300 bars; each summarized by analysis/indicators.summarize_timeframe (close, sma20, sma50, rsi, trend in {bullish,bearish,neutral} from close vs SMA50 + SMA20 vs SMA50).
- "signals" = crypto_signals(sym, interval) (servers/crypto.py:118): 500 bars at interval; analysis/indicators.signals_from_frame detects golden_cross/death_cross (SMA50 x SMA200), rsi_oversold(<30)/rsi_overbought(>70), macd_bullish/bearish_cross, bollinger_break_up/down, plus latest "values".
- "derivatives" = derivatives(sym, interval) (servers/crypto.py:153): four independent registry fetches — capabilities "funding_rate", "open_interest" (with history), "long_short_ratio", "crypto_ohlcv" (limit=48). Derives: basis (funding payload basis or basis_value(mark,index)=mark/index-1, analysis/crypto_metrics.py:130 — surfaced, NOT scored to avoid double-counting funding), oi_change_pct (oi_change_from_history: last/first-1 across OI history rows, crypto_metrics.py:137), price_change_pct (closes[-1]/closes[0]-1 over the 48-bar window).
- "sentiment" = crypto_sentiment() (servers/crypto.py:202): registry "crypto_sentiment" (Fear & Greed, providers/fear_greed.py) + "crypto_global" (total cap / BTC dominance, providers/coingecko.py). The global block is carried for context, NOT scored.
- "factors" = _compute_crypto_factors (crypto.py:117): crypto_ohlcv 500 bars -> analysis/factors.factor_values(bars, periods_per_year=365*1440/interval_minutes) -> n_bars, last_close, atr14 (analysis/risk.atr period 14), momentum_12_1 (close[-21]/close[-252]-1, BAR-based windows on intraday), pct_52w_high (252-bar high), trend_200 (close/SMA200-1), realized_vol (126-bar log-ret stdev annualized). No fundamentals for crypto so value/quality keys absent.
PROVIDERS: crypto_ohlcv / funding_rate / open_interest / long_short_ratio served by providers/binance.py (primary) with providers/bybit.py fallback; crypto_sentiment by providers/fear_greed.py (alternative.me); crypto_global by providers/coingecko.py. All routed through the singleton ProviderRegistry (providers/__init__.get_registry; auto-registration via @register_provider on module import, listed in _PROVIDER_MODULES tuple at providers/__init__.py:31). servers/crypto.get_registry() is the module-level monkeypatch seam for offline tests.

(2) SCORING. `score_crypto_evidence(dossier) -> dict` (crypto.py:219), pure; same output shape as equity debate.score_evidence: {factors:[{category,name,side,contribution,detail}], net_score, bull_score, bear_score, categories, divergence_penalty:0.0, n_factors}. Each factor built by debate._factor(category,name,contribution,detail) (debate.py:187); contribution = strength_in[-1,1] x weight. Complete factor/weight list:
- TECHNICAL SIGNALS (reused debate._score_signals, debate.py:199; category "technical"): per-signal weights golden_cross/death_cross +/-2.0, macd_bullish/bearish_cross +/-1.5, rsi_oversold/overbought +/-1.0, bollinger_break_up/down +/-1.0, default 1.0 (full weight, not scaled — binary signals).
- PRICE FACTORS (reused debate._score_factors, debate.py:406, with crypto saturations from factor_saturations(interval), crypto.py:96): momentum_12_1 -> clamp(mom/mom_sat)*2.0 (_FACTOR_MOMENTUM_WEIGHT, category "momentum"); trend_200 -> clamp(trend/trend_sat)*1.5 (category "trend"); pct_52w_high -> clamp((p52-(1-band))/band)*1.0 (category "momentum"). Saturations anchored at crypto 1d values (mom 0.60, trend 0.20, p52 band 0.30) scaled by sqrt(interval_minutes/1440) with floors 0.01/0.004/0.005 — e.g. 15m: ~0.0612/0.0204/0.0306. Equity value/quality branches are dead for crypto (no fundamentals keys).
- MTF (_score_mtf, crypto.py:166; category "trend", name "mtf_alignment"): strength=(bull-bear)/decided over the 5m/15m/1h trend labels x _MTF_WEIGHT=1.5; skipped on tie or zero decided.
- DERIVATIVES (_score_derivatives, crypto.py:184): funding_signal(rate, annualized) (crypto_metrics.py:55) = -clamp((annualized - baseline)/0.50, -1, 1) where baseline=+0.01%/8h annualized (~+10.95%); contrarian, centered so resting funding scores ~0; x _FUNDING_WEIGHT=2.0, category "funding". oi_price_signal(oi_change_pct, price_change_pct) (crypto_metrics.py:76): mag=clamp(|px|/0.02); OI rising -> price_dir*mag (confirm); OI falling -> price_dir*mag*(-0.3) (fade); x _OI_WEIGHT=2.0, category "open_interest". long_short_signal(ratio) (crypto_metrics.py:98) = -clamp(ln(ratio)/ln(3.0), -1, 1) (contrarian, 3:1 = full); x _LS_WEIGHT=1.0, category "positioning".
- SENTIMENT (_score_crypto_sentiment, crypto.py:207): fear_greed_signal(value) (crypto_metrics.py:108): >=75 ramps bearish to -1 at 100; <=25 ramps bullish to +1 at 0; 25..75 = 0 (extremes only, contrarian); x _FNG_WEIGHT=1.0, category "sentiment".
DECISION MAPPING (shared debate.decide_from_scores -> _quant_decision, debate.py:547): conviction = (|net|/(bull+bear)) * clamp(n_categories/4,0,1) * (1-0.3*divergence_penalty[always 0 for crypto]); BUY if net>=+1.0, SHORT if net<=-1.0, else AVOID; also AVOID if conviction<0.10 or not corroborated (need >=2 distinct categories OR |net|>=2.0). suggested_sizing: >=0.6 moderate / >=0.35 small / else minimal. bull_case/bear_case/rationale fall back to factor details. Result is a TradeDecision (core/types.py:541) with action/direction/conviction/net_score/bull_score/bear_score/factors/method="quant"/data_quality/DISCLAIMER; crypto-specific fields asset_class ("crypto"), leverage (dict), sizing, regime, horizon set by enrichment.

(3) LEVERAGE PLAN. `async enrich_crypto_decision(decision, dossier, *, interval, leverage_cap, settings)` (crypto.py:259): (a) regime = analysis/crypto_regime.crypto_regime() — BTCUSDT 1d 400 bars + FNG; crypto_regime_from_bars (crypto_regime.py:38): risk_on (BTC>200d SMA AND 12-1 mom>0, gross 1.0) / caution (one, 0.6) / risk_off (none, 0.3); vol overlay clamp(target_vol 0.80 / realized_vol(365-annualized), 0.4, 1.0); FNG trim x0.85 when >=80 or <=20; gross_exposure = base*vol_scale*fng_scale; never raises (fallback caution). (b) funding rate + interval_hours pulled from dossier["derivatives"]["funding"]. (c) horizon/hold from _HORIZON_BY_INTERVAL (crypto.py:55; 15m -> ("intraday", 6.0h); 1m-5m scalp, 2h/4h swing, 1d position 96h). (d) analysis/leverage.leverage_plan(price=factors.last_close, atr_value=factors.atr14, direction, conviction, funding_rate, funding_interval_hours, expected_hold_hours, max_leverage=leverage_cap or settings.crypto_max_leverage [20.0, env MCP_CRYPTO_MAX_LEVERAGE], risk_per_trade=settings.crypto_risk_per_trade [0.025], mmr=settings.crypto_maint_margin_rate [0.005], liq_buffer=settings.crypto_liq_buffer [0.5], regime_scale=gross) (leverage.py:121). Math: stop_frac = clamp(2.0*ATR14/price, 1e-4, 0.95) (fallback 0.02 if no ATR); max_safe_leverage = clamp(1/(stop_frac*(1+liq_buffer)+mmr), 1, hard_cap) (liquidation distance ~1/L - mmr must exceed stop by buffer); suggested_leverage = clamp(0.5*max_safe, 1, max_leverage) (DEFAULT_LEVERAGE_FRACTION=0.5 — half the ceiling for wick cushion); risk_used = risk_per_trade*(0.4+0.6*clamp(conviction,0,1))*clamp(regime_scale,0,1); notional_pct = risk_used/stop_frac; margin_pct = notional_pct/suggested_leverage; stop_price = price -/+ stop_frac*price (long/short); target_price = price +/- 2.0*stop_frac*price (r_multiple=2); liquidation_price = entry*(1 - 1/L + mmr) long, entry*(1 + 1/L - mmr) short (leverage.py:51); funding_cost = rate*(hold_hours/interval_hours)*(+1 long / -1 short), est_funding_cost_pct = funding_cost*notional_pct; FLAT/invalid direction -> all-zero plan. Plan stored on decision.leverage; stop/target/margin_pct/r_multiple/regime_scale mirrored into decision.sizing; decision.invalidation defaulted to "price through stop X (or liquidation Y)".

(4) SCREEN. `async screen_crypto(*, symbols=None, interval="15m", shortlist=10, top_n=3, universe_limit=40, leverage_cap=None, force_refresh=False, hyperliquid_only=True, settings=None) -> MarketScreen` (orchestration/crypto_screen.py:149). Universe: explicit symbols (canonicalized) OR core/crypto_universe.fetch_top_perps (live Binance /fapi/v1/ticker/24hr ranked by quoteVolume -> Bybit /v5/market/tickers turnover24h fallback -> JSON cache crypto_universe.json, TTL 24h, depth 200 -> curated MAJOR_CRYPTO_BASES (core/symbols.py:26, 40 majors) as BASEUSDT offline fallback; sources tagged live/cache/fallback; sliced to limit). hyperliquid_only filter (_filter_to_hyperliquid, crypto_screen.py:125): fetch_hyperliquid_perps (keyless POST {"type":"meta"} to settings.hyperliquid_testnet_url /info; cache hyperliquid_perps.json TTL 24h; NO curated fallback — "unavailable" -> don't filter) keeps symbols whose base_asset(s) is in the exchange coin set; skipped if it would empty the universe. Stage 1 prefilter (prefilter_universe, crypto_screen.py:70; semaphore 8): 300 bars/name -> factor_values -> screen.prefilter_score (orchestration/screen.py:71) = clamp(mom/sat)*2.0 + clamp(trend/sat)*1.5 + clamp((p52-(1-band))/band)*1.0 using the same crypto factor_saturations(interval); None (insufficient history) -> error row; ranked desc. Shortlist: top `shortlist` entries with score>0 (longs) + most-negative `shortlist` with score<0 (shorts), deduped. Stage 2 deep dive (_deep_dive, semaphore 5): full decide_crypto per name. Ranking: BUY decisions sorted by (-conviction, -net_score), SHORT by (-conviction, net_score), each sliced to top_n. Returns MarketScreen (core/types.py:703) universe="Crypto perps" with regime, top_longs/top_shorts as full TradeDecision dicts (leverage plan included), long_shortlist/short_shortlist transparency entries, errors capped at 25, summary + DISCLAIMER. Never raises.

SYMBOLS (core/symbols.py): canonical_crypto -> joined BASEQUOTE with USD-like quotes mapped to USDT (BTC/BTC-USD/$btc -> BTCUSDT); base_asset/split_crypto; to_hyperliquid_coin = bare base; is_crypto_symbol heuristic (separator, USDT/USDC/BUSD/PERP suffix, or major base). MCP surface: servers/crypto.py exposes the 6 read tools via create_sdk_mcp_server(name="crypto"); decide_crypto/screen_crypto are exposed upstream by mcp_server (decide_crypto prompt / decide_crypto_market prompt run the host-side bull/bear debate; a host verdict can be merged via decide_from_scores(verdict=...) which overrides action/conviction/horizon while preserving quant factors).

extension_points.how: Create a Provider subclass decorated with @register_provider declaring supported={"<new_capability>"} (e.g. "crypto_social", "crypto_news", "orderbook"), lazy-import httpx inside fetchers, return a core value type .to_dict() payload with Provenance; add the module name to the _PROVIDER_MODULES tuple so its registration side effect runs (import failures are silently skipped).
extension_points.notes: Registry gives caching/rate-limit/fallback for free; keyless-first preferred. Add a value dataclass to core/types.py (pattern: FundingRate/LongShortRatio — to_dict(), Provenance field) if the payload has structure.

extension_points.how: Add a pure async logic function (pattern: derivatives()/crypto_sentiment() at servers/crypto.py:153/202) that calls _fetch_data(get_registry(), "<capability>", ...) tolerantly ({"_error":...} per metric), derives any composite values, returns a plain dict with DISCLAIMER; then a thin @tool wrapper returning text_result(result) and append it to the server=create_sdk_mcp_server(tools=[...]) list at servers/crypto.py:292.
extension_points.notes: Keep the module-level get_registry() seam so tests can monkeypatch. Do not import other servers — share logic via analysis/ only.

extension_points.how: Add one entry to the tasks dict, e.g. tasks["social"] = cx.crypto_social(sym) (or new server module). asyncio.gather(return_exceptions=True) already converts failures to {"_error":...}; the dossier automatically carries the new key.
extension_points.notes: No other change needed for the data to reach scoring/tests; keep the call keyless-capable or tolerant when keys are absent.

extension_points.how: Add a pure signal core `def <domain>_signal(...) -> tuple[float, str] | None` returning (strength in [-1,1], detail string), with module-level saturation/baseline constants (pattern: funding_signal at line 55 — de-mean against a structural baseline, clamp at a researched saturation, return None when uncomputable).
extension_points.notes: Keep it pure/offline-testable; document the research basis in the docstring per existing convention. Beware double-counting correlated metrics (basis vs funding precedent at lines 22-25).

extension_points.how: Define a weight constant next to _MTF_WEIGHT.._FNG_WEIGHT (lines 48-52), write _score_<domain>(dossier, factors) that reads the new dossier key, calls the analysis signal core, and appends _factor("<category>", "<name>", strength * _WEIGHT, detail); call it inside score_crypto_evidence after _score_crypto_sentiment. The factor then flows automatically into net/bull/bear scores, the categories corroboration set, conviction, decide_from_scores, TradeDecision.factors, bull_case/bear_case fallbacks, and rationale.
extension_points.notes: Choose a NEW category string (e.g. "social", "news", "orderflow") — n_categories>=2 is the corroboration gate and feeds coverage=n/4 in conviction, so adding categories slightly inflates coverage; consider whether the /4 divisor in debate._quant_conviction (debate.py:533) and data_quality coverage (debate.py:673) should grow.

extension_points.how: Nothing required — the deep dive calls decide_crypto so new factors flow into screen rankings automatically. Only touch it if the new domain should join the cheap stage-1 prefilter (extend _prefilter_factors/_shortlist_entry and screen.prefilter_score).
extension_points.notes: Stage 1 is deliberately one kline pull per name; keep expensive/keyed sources in stage 2.

extension_points.how: Unit-test the analysis signal core purely; test the server logic fn by monkeypatching servers.crypto.get_registry; test scoring by feeding a hand-built dossier dict to score_crypto_evidence; screen tests monkeypatch _prefilter_factors/decide_crypto/fetch_top_perps/fetch_hyperliquid_perps.
extension_points.notes: pytest, asyncio_mode=auto, no network; run .venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q.

conventions: Import-safe: no network or heavy imports at module import; pandas/ta/httpx/SDK lazy-imported inside functions; provider modules registered via decorator side effects with try/except wrapping.

Tolerant evidence gathering: every fetch failure becomes an {"_error": ...} marker under its dossier key (asyncio.gather(return_exceptions=True) or per-metric try/except); orchestration entry points (decide_crypto, screen_crypto, crypto_regime) never raise.

Strict layering: providers -> servers -> orchestration; servers never import each other, shared math lives in analysis/; servers expose pure async logic fns + thin @tool wrappers + module-level get_registry() monkeypatch seam.

Pure deterministic scoring: signal cores return (strength in [-1,1], detail) tuples; engine multiplies by module-level weight constants; contrarian signals de-meaned against structural baselines (funding +0.01%/8h, FNG extremes-only); saturations scale ~sqrt(interval/1d) with floors.

Shared decision mapper: decide_from_scores is asset-agnostic; crypto reuses equity _factor/_score_signals/_score_factors; output shape of score_crypto_evidence intentionally identical to score_evidence.
AI-free engine: the bull/bear debate runs in the MCP host via prompts; host verdicts merge through decide_from_scores(verdict=...) without discarding quant factors.
Every user-facing output carries DISCLAIMER; sizing is percentage-of-budget, never dollars; CLI output strictly ASCII.

Caching: registry-level TTL cache for capability fetches; file-based 24h JSON caches for universes (crypto_universe.json, hyperliquid_perps.json) with live -> cache -> fallback resolution and source tagging; blocking universe fetches run via asyncio.to_thread.
Symbols canonicalized at every public boundary (canonical_crypto -> BASEUSDT; Hyperliquid uses bare base via to_hyperliquid_coin/base_asset).

Config via Settings.from_env() with MCP_-prefixed env overrides (MCP_CRYPTO_MAX_LEVERAGE, MCP_CRYPTO_RISK_PER_TRADE, MCP_CRYPTO_MAINT_MARGIN, MCP_CRYPTO_TARGET_VOL, MCP_CRYPTO_LIQ_BUFFER); offline tests with mocked seams (get_registry, _build_clients).
gaps: No persistence or state between runs: decide_crypto/screen_crypto are stateless one-shots; no decision journal, no open-position awareness in the decision engine (a swarm re-deciding a symbol it already holds gets no 'you are long from X' context; portfolio state lives only on the Hyperliquid testnet account).

No event/streaming layer: everything is poll-based REST through a TTL cache; no websocket feeds, no funding-settlement or liquidation-cascade triggers, no change-detection to wake agents — an always-on swarm must build its own scheduler/poller and dedupe.

No cross-agent coordination primitives: no locking or idempotency between concurrent deciders (two agents screening simultaneously can both decide to size the full risk budget into correlated perps); risk_per_trade is per-trade with no portfolio-level aggregate exposure, correlation, or max-concurrent-positions control (regime gross_exposure scales each trade, not the book).

No decision TTL/staleness contract: a TradeDecision carries no as_of/expiry; on 15m bars a verdict can be invalid within minutes; no re-validation hook before execution (paper_trade_decision re-decides, but manually placed plans can go stale).

Evidence domains are narrow: no orderbook depth/imbalance, no liquidation/CVD data, no social sentiment, no news/catalyst feed, no on-chain flows; FNG is market-wide not per-coin; long/short ratio availability varies by provider; divergence_penalty is hardwired 0.0 for crypto (the equity cross-check has no crypto analogue).

Scoring weights/saturations are hand-tuned constants with no calibration loop: no outcome tracking, no per-factor hit-rate telemetry, no walk-forward re-weighting; the backtest module is not wired into the crypto engine for parameter feedback.

No exit/management engine: the plan emits static stop/target/liquidation at entry; nothing monitors positions for stop-hit, trailing, funding-flip, or regime-flip exits — an always-on swarm needs a position-manager agent built on paper_account/paper_orders/paper_close.

Conviction coverage divisor (n_categories/4) and the >=2-category corroboration gate are tuned for the current category set; adding evidence domains silently shifts conviction semantics across all agents unless recalibrated.

Rate-limit budget is per-process: multiple swarm processes sharing Binance/Bybit/CoinGecko keyless quotas have no shared limiter; the registry singleton and file caches are per-process with no cross-process locking on cache files beyond atomic replace.

Liquidation/MMR model is simplified (flat mmr=0.005, no tiers/fees) and the universe symbology is Binance/Bybit USDT-perp centric while execution is on Hyperliquid (k-prefixed coins deliberately under-matched) — fine for suggestions, but a swarm sizing real margin needs venue-exact specs (Hyperliquid max_leverage per coin is fetched in fetch_hyperliquid_perps but unused by leverage_plan).
